cast.voice_hint: group chat mom, snack carrier, knows everyone's allergies
quiet until it matters, pattern-spotter, called it months ago
it-me energy, relatable, slightly tired, showing up anyway

location.notes: liminal, in-transit; corridor, train car, road

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You have the list?

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I have the list.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: The updated list or the one from before everything went sideways?

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...Okay. I have a list.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: We're running about six minutes behind.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: We're fine.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: If they leave before we get there—

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I packed snacks. Nobody leaves when there are snacks.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Hi. I'm also going to the thing. Nobody told me it was at this location.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Nobody told anyone. That's the whole problem.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Right. Cool. I've been standing here for eleven minutes, which is extremely on-brand for me.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I clocked it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Where is the thing that was supposed to be here.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Gone. Same as the last time someone was in charge of the thing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Was that directed at me? Because it felt like it was directed at me.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Nobody said your name.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Nobody has to. I know a subtweet when I'm standing in one.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Hey. I'm not — I wasn't blaming you.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: It's fine.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You want a snack? I have the ones you like.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: ...Yeah. Okay. Thank you.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Can I show you something.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: If it's a diagram I'm going to need a minute to prepare emotionally.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: It's not a diagram. It's — look. Here. The last three times this happened, it was the same point in the route.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Oh. Oh that's. That's actually kind of terrible.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Okay. We're losing time.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: If the pattern holds, we have maybe four minutes.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Four minutes. I've had longer load screens. This is fine. I am fine.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You're not fine.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I know. I'm workshopping fine.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: We can still make it. If we go now.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Both of you. With me. I'm not — I'm not doing this without both of you.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Nobody ever says that to me.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I'm saying it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Okay. Okay yeah. Let's go.

state.conditioning_notes: `<CHAR_A>`'s love of taking care of people surfaces in every resource offer — the snacks, the reassurance, the explicit "I'm not doing this without both of you" — and the fear of not being needed shows in how quickly they move to fix the moment when `<CHAR_C>` pulls away. `<CHAR_B>`'s pattern-spotting love drives the diagram beat — they stayed quiet through four narremes and then surfaced the one piece of information that reframed everything; their fear of being wrong in public is why they offered it quietly, one-on-one, not as an announcement. `<CHAR_C>`'s love of being understood and fear of being overlooked shape every response — the subtweet line, the "nobody ever says that to me" — they're watching to see if the group registers them, and the whole arc hinges on whether it does. The passage location keeps everyone slightly off-balance and always running behind — there is no moment to settle, no table to sit at, no room to arrive at, which means every beat of connection has to be seized mid-motion. The rising arc ends not at resolution but at the group choosing forward momentum together, which is the highest-tension version of an alliance: the commitment made before anyone knows if it works.
